title: baseline resolution failure escapes the command-result contract
reasoning: runFenceCommand exposes a FenceCommandResult-returning API, and auditFence handles later operational failures by returning exitCode/stdout/stderr. However the initial git rev-parse for the configured baseline runs before the try/catch. If the baseline ref is missing or git errors, run() can throw out of auditFence, so callers get a rejected promise instead of the documented command result shape. That is inconsistent with the rest of the runtime error handling and can crash the CLI path instead of producing a controlled failure.
reproduction: Configure an invalid baseline ref, then call runFenceCommand([]). The rev-parse at lines 109-115 executes before the catch block starts, so the thrown error bypasses the FenceCommandResult return path.
recommendation: Move baseline command planning and baselineSha resolution inside the existing try/catch, or add a narrow try/catch around rev-parse that returns a FenceCommandResult with exitCode 1 and stderr.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The included tests only exercise pure mutation-core exports from @codenuke/fence. They do not import ./runtime or cover auditFence/runFenceCommand error paths.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a runtime test that stubs or configures an invalid baseline and asserts runFenceCommand([]) resolves to { exitCode: 1, stderr: ... } rather than rejecting.
minimumFixScope: packages/fence/src/main/runtime.ts auditFence baseline resolution error handling
